---
title: "Automating iLand on HPC"
subtitle: "From hard-coded scripts to parallel, replicated scenario runs"
author: "Quinn Asena · CPCRW iLand project"
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: dark
    highlight-style: monokai
    slide-number: c/t
    transition: slide
    background-transition: fade
    code-block-height: 520px
    smaller: true
---
## The starting point
Two files drove the workflow — both full of hard-coded paths:
::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}
**JavaScript file** `afterFireProcessing()`
```javascript
Fire.gridToFile(
  'crownkill',
  '/glade/derecho/scratch/hayesk/output/' +
  'CPCRW_nofecund/crownkill/crownkill_'
  + pid + '_' + praefix + '.txt'
);
```
:::
::: {.column width="50%"}
**Bash script** `run_iland_replicates.sh`
```bash
/glade/work/hayesk/iLand2.0/iland-model/ \
  build/ilandc/ilandc \
  /glade/work/hayesk/CPCRW_fecund/$dirname.xml \
  $years …
mkdir /glade/derecho/scratch/hayesk/ \
  output/${dirname}/crownkill_$dirname.$rep
```
:::
:::
. . .
::: {.callout-warning}
A new project file needed a new `.js` file, new paths everywhere, and running
in parallel caused **race conditions**.
:::
---
## Step 1 — Remove hard-coding from `afterFireProcessing()`
Replace the literal path with iLand's own settings API:
::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}
❌ Before
```javascript
function afterFireProcessing() {
  var praefix = Globals.year;
  var pid = Fire.id;
  Fire.gridToFile(
    'crownkill',
    '/glade/derecho/scratch/hayesk/output/' +
    'CPCRW_nofecund/crownkill/crownkill_'
    + pid + '_' + praefix + '.txt'
  );
}
```
:::
::: {.column width="50%"}
✅ After
```javascript
function afterFireProcessing() {
  var outputPath = Globals.path(
    Globals.setting('system.path.output')
  );
  var praefix = Globals.year;
  var pid = Fire.id;
  Fire.gridToFile(
    'crownkill',
    outputPath + '/crownkill/crownkill_'
    + pid + '_' + praefix + '.txt'
  );
}
```
:::
:::
. . .
::: {.callout-note}
One `.js` file now works for *any* project — the path comes from the XML at
runtime via `Globals.setting('system.path.output')`.
:::
---
## Step 2 — CSV-based scenario & replicate approach
Define every scenario combination once in a CSV:
```csv
fecund,gcm,fri,epsilon,dbh,id
species_param_pa01pl035be05pr07,NorEsm2-MMssp126V3,120,2.7,2.5,filt_1980
```
. . .
The bash script reads it line by line:
```bash
# call: bash run_iland.sh project.xml 1 5
xml=$1
start_rep=$2
end_rep=$3
csv_name="iland_reps.csv"
sed 1d "$csv_name" | while IFS=, read -r fecund gcm fri epsilon dbh id
do
    for rep in $(seq "$start_rep" "$end_rep")
    do
        echo "running gcm $gcm fecund $fecund rep $rep"
        # … scenario logic …
    done
done
```
. . .
::: {.callout-tip}
Adding a new scenario = adding one row to the CSV. No script edits needed.
:::
---
## Step 3 — Create directory structure in the bash script
Each replicate gets its own isolated output tree:
```bash
scenario_dir="${output_path}/${gcm}_${fecund}_ep${epsilon}_dbh${dbh}_${id}/rep_${rep}"
mkdir -p "${scenario_dir}/crownkill"   # fire crown-kill grids  (.txt)
mkdir -p "${scenario_dir}/nFire"       # fire count grids       (.asc)
mkdir -p "${scenario_dir}/log"         # iLand log files
```
. . .
::: {.callout-note}
`mkdir -p` creates the full path and is silent if it already exists —
safe to call inside nested loops.
:::
. . .
::: {.callout-tip}
Outputs for every scenario × replicate combination land in their own folder,
never mixed together.
:::
---
## Step 4 — Remove hard-coded bash dependencies
All site-specific paths live as variables at the top of the script:
```bash
# ── configure these for your HPC environment ──────────────
path="/glade/work/qasena/iLand2.0/iland-model/build/ilandc/ilandc"
output_path="/glade/derecho/scratch/qasena/output_auto/CPCRW_hist_spinup"
xml_path="/glade/work/qasena/iLand_automated"
simulation_years="300"
csv_name="/glade/work/qasena/iLand_automated/iland_reps.csv"
# ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
```
. . .
iLand is then called with fully-expanded variables:
```bash
"${path}" "$tmp_xml" "$simulation_years" \
    system.database.out=${gcm}_${fecund}_${rep}.sqlite \
    system.database.climate=${gcm}.sqlite \
    system.database.in=${fecund}.sqlite \
    system.logging.logFile=${scenario_dir}/log/log_${gcm}_${fecund}_${rep}.txt \
    modules.fire.fireReturnInterval=${fri} \
    model.settings.epsilon=${epsilon} \
    output.saplingdetail.minDbh=${dbh}
```
---
## Step 5 — Redirecting iLand's internal output to scratch
iLand resolves all output paths relative to the `<output>` tag in its project
XML. On HPC, outputs *must* land on a fast scratch filesystem, not on the work
filesystem where the XML lives.
```xml
<!-- default — output stays next to the project file -->
<output>output</output>
<!-- what we need — an absolute scratch path per replicate -->
<output>/glade/derecho/scratch/qasena/output_auto/…/rep_1</output>
```
. . .
::: {.callout-note}
Overriding this tag is what allows the `.js` file to resolve
`Globals.setting('system.path.output')` to the right scratch directory at
runtime — without touching the `.js` file at all.
:::
---
## The race condition problem
First attempt: let all replicates write to a *shared* crownkill directory,
then `cp`/`rm` into per-replicate folders.
```bash
# ❌ Original approach — breaks with parallel instances
"${path}" ${xml} $years \
    system.database.out=output_${rep}.sqlite …
cp -r ${output_path}/crownkill/* \
      ${scenario_dir}/crownkill_${gcm}_${fecund}_${rep}/
rm    ${output_path}/crownkill/*
```
. . .
::: {.callout-warning}
When multiple iLand instances run simultaneously they all write fire grids to
the **same** `crownkill/` directory. The `cp` of rep 1 can pick up files from
rep 2, and the `rm` can delete files that rep 3 hasn't copied yet.
:::
. . .
→ Files from different replicates get mixed together or lost entirely.
---
## Final solution — copy XML & overwrite `<output>`
Give each replicate its *own temporary XML* pointing to its own output directory:
```bash
scenario_dir="${output_path}/${gcm}_${fecund}_ep${epsilon}_dbh${dbh}_${id}/rep_${rep}"
tmp_xml="${xml_path}/${gcm}_${fecund}_ep${epsilon}_dbh${dbh}_${id}_${rep}.xml"
# 1. copy the base project XML
cp "$xml" "$tmp_xml"
# 2. rewrite the <output> tag to an absolute, replicate-specific path
sed -i "s|<output>.*</output>|<output>${scenario_dir}</output>|" "$tmp_xml"
# 3. run iLand with the patched XML
"${path}" "$tmp_xml" "$simulation_years" …
# 4. clean up the temporary XML
rm "$tmp_xml"
```
. . .
::: {.callout-tip}
✅ Each iLand instance writes its fire grids directly to its own
`scenario_dir/crownkill/` — no shared directory, no race conditions.
:::
---
## Summary
| Problem | Solution |
|---|---|
| Hard-coded paths in `.js` file | Read output path from `Globals.setting('system.path.output')` |
| Hard-coded paths in bash script | Variables declared at the top; passed via CSV columns |
| One bash call per project file | CSV-driven loop — add a row, not a script |
| Directory creation was manual | `mkdir -p` inside the replicate loop |
| Race conditions (shared crownkill dir) | Per-replicate temporary XML with unique `<output>` path |
| iLand output locked to work filesystem | `sed` rewrites `<output>` to absolute scratch path |
. . .
::: {.callout-tip}
Result: fully parallel, replicated scenario runs with zero shared state between
instances — on any HPC with a scratch filesystem.
:::
